---
title: "Backtest Results"
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from analysis.breakout_strategy import run_analysis
from IPython.display import display

analysis = run_analysis()
display(analysis["metrics_display"])
```

## What The Metrics Mean

```{python}
from IPython.display import Markdown

metric_notes = "\n".join(
    f"- **{row.Metric}:** {row.Explanation}"
    for row in analysis["metric_explanations"].itertuples(index=False)
)
display(Markdown(metric_notes))
```

## Strategy Performance

This page is scaffolded for the final performance visuals and tables. It will eventually compare:

- baseline breakout results
- ML-filtered breakout results
- trade outcome distributions
- equity and drawdown behavior

## Trade Outcome Analysis

```{python}
display(analysis["outcome_summary"])
```

## Interpretation

The finished version of this page should answer whether the breakout rules produce a tradable return stream after realistic costs, and whether the supervised learning filter improves trade quality rather than just overfitting.
